steps.title: Find the zeros
steps.content: Factor \(x^2 - 42x + 80\): two numbers that multiply to 80 and add to \(-42\) are \(-2\) and \(-40\). The zeros are \(x = 2\) and \(x = 40\).

steps.title: Scan the choices for 2 and 40 as visible literals
steps.content:
A: \(x^2 - 5x - 37x + 80\) — splits the middle term; no zero visible as a standalone constant.
B: \((x-2)(x-40)\) — 2 and 40 appear explicitly as constants. ✓
C: \(x(x-42)+80\) — 42 and 80 are visible but neither is a zero of the function.
D: \((x-21)^2 - 361\) — vertex form; zeros require computing \(21 \pm \sqrt{361}\), so they are hidden.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
